title: Pipedream Project
description: Project that can be accessed via the Connect API

app_name.description: App name shown to Connect users

support_email.description: Support email configured for the project

connect_require_key_auth_test.description: Send a test request to the upstream API when adding Connect accounts for key-based apps

AccountId.description: The unique ID of the account.

Account.description: End user account data, returned from the API.

name.description: The custom name of the account if set.

external_id.description: The external ID associated with the account.

healthy.description: Indicates if the account is healthy. Pipedream will periodically retry token refresh and test requests for unhealthy accounts

dead.description: Indicates if the account is no longer active

created_at.description: The date and time the account was created, an ISO 8601 formatted string

updated_at.description: The date and time the account was last updated, an ISO 8601 formatted string

authorized_scopes.description: The OAuth scopes effectively granted to this account. Empty for non-OAuth apps.

credentials.description: The credentials associated with the account, if the `include_credentials` parameter was set to true in the request (only applicable for BYOA apps). In addition to the well-known OAuth fields listed below, this object may contain app-specific custom fields (e.g. `base_url`).

oauth_client_id.description: The OAuth client ID for the app, if applicable

oauth_signer_uri.description: The OAuth signer URI, if the app uses a custom OAuth signer

expires_at.description: The date and time the account's credentials expiration, an ISO 8601 formatted string

error.description: The error message if the account is unhealthy or dead, null otherwise

last_refreshed_at.description: The date and time the account was last refreshed, an ISO 8601 formatted string

next_refresh_at.description: The date and time the account will next be refreshed, an ISO 8601 formatted string

App.description: Response object for a Pipedream app's metadata

id.description: ID of the app. Only applies for OAuth apps.

name_slug.description: The name slug of the target app (see https://pipedream.com/docs/connect/quickstart#find-your-apps-name-slug)

name.description: The human-readable name of the app

description.description: A short description of the app

img_src.description: The URL to the app's logo

custom_fields_json.description: A JSON string representing the custom fields for the app

featured_weight.description: A rough directional ordering of app popularity, subject to changes by Pipedream

AppAuthType.description: The authentication type used by the app

AppCategory.description: Response object for a Pipedream app category

name.description: The human-readable name of the app category

description.description: A short description of the app category

action_run_credits_used.description: Credits used when running Connect actions

proxy_credits_used.description: Credits used by Connect proxy requests

source_emit_credits_used.description: Credits used by Connect source event emissions

ConnectUsageResponse.description: Connect usage records for a time window

BackendClientOpts.description: Options for creating a server-side client

scope.description: Optional space-separated scopes for the access token. Defaults to `*`.

ClientOpts.description: Options for creating a server-side client

api_url.description: The API URL to use (defaults to https://api.pipedream.com/)

key.description: The key that uniquely identifies the component.

name.description: The human-readable name of the component, e.g. 'GitLab: List Commits'

version.description: The latest version of the component, in SemVer format.

component_type.description: The type of component (trigger or action)

destructiveHint.description: If true, the component may perform destructive updates to its environment. If false, the component performs only additive updates.

idempotentHint.description: If true, calling the component repeatedly with the same arguments will have no additional effect on the its environment.

openWorldHint.description: If true, this component may interact with an “open world” of external entities. If false, the component's domain of interaction is closed. For example, the world of a web search component is open, whereas that of a memory component is not.

readOnlyHint.description: If true, the component does not modify its environment.

title.description: A human-readable title for the component.

ComponentStash.description: Indicates if a File Stash ID is optional or required to run the component

ComponentType.description: The type of component (trigger or action)

TimerInterval.description: Timer configuration using interval in seconds

intervalSeconds.description: Interval in seconds for timer execution

ConfigurablePropBase.description: A configuration or input field for a component.

name.description: When building `configuredProps`, make sure to use this field as the key when setting the prop value

label.description: Value to use as an input label. In cases where `type` is "app", should load the app via `getApp`, etc. and show `app.name` instead.

description.description: A description of the prop, shown to the user when configuring the component.

optional.description: If true, this prop does not need to be specified.

disabled.description: If true, this prop will be ignored.

readOnly.description: If true, this prop is read-only — its value is either fixed by the component author (`static`) or the prop is purely informational (e.g. `alert`, `dir`). Connect clients should render it without treating it as a configurable input.

hidden.description: If true, should not expose this prop to the user

remoteOptions.description: If true, call `configureComponent` for this prop to load remote options. It is safe, and preferred, given a returned list of { label: string; value: any } objects to set the prop value to { __lv: { label: string; value: any } }. This way, on load, you can access label for the value without necessarily reloading these options

useQuery.description: If true, calls to `configureComponent` for this prop support receiving a `query` parameter to filter remote options

reloadProps.description: If true, after setting a value for this prop, a call to `reloadComponentProps` is required as the component has dynamic configurable props dependent on this one

withLabel.description: If true, you must save the configured prop value as a "label-value" object which should look like: { __lv: { label: string; value: any } } because the execution needs to access the label

allOf.description: This prop is used to configure an alert component

content.description: The content of the alert, which can include HTML or plain text.

ConfigurablePropAlertType.description: The severity level of the alert.

allOf.description: This prop can accept any value.
This prop is used to configure an account for a specific app

app.description: The name slug of the app, e.g. 'github', 'slack', etc. This is used to identify the app for which the account is being configured.

allOf.description: This prop can accept a boolean value.
This prop is used to configure a timer interface.

HttpRequestField.description: A name-value field for HTTP request configuration

HttpRequestConfig.description: Configuration for an HTTP request prop

allOf.description: This prop is used to configure an app webhook interface.

appProp.description: The name of the app prop that this apphook depends on

eventNames.description: List of event names to listen for

allOf.description: This prop can accept an array of integers.

min.description: The minimum value for integers in this array

max.description: The maximum value for integers in this array

options.description: Available options for the integer array

allOf.description: This prop is used to configure an HTTP interface.

customResponse.description: Whether this HTTP interface allows custom responses

allOf.description: This prop is used to configure a database service.
This prop is used to configure a data store for key-value storage.
This prop is used to indicate how a component may access File Stash

sync.description: If true, the component's /tmp directory is synchronized with File Stash

ConfigurablePropDirAccessMode.description: The mode in which the component may access File Stash

allOf.description: This prop is used to configure an HTTP request with URL, method, headers, params, body, and authentication.
This prop is used to configure SQL queries.

app.description: The app that provides SQL authentication

allOf.description: This prop is used to select an Airtable base.

appProp.description: The name of the app prop that provides Airtable authentication

allOf.description: This prop is used to select an Airtable table.

baseIdProp.description: The name of the prop that provides the Airtable base ID

allOf.description: This prop is used to select an Airtable view.

tableIdProp.description: The name of the prop that provides the Airtable table ID

allOf.description: This prop is used to select an Airtable field.

tableIdProp.description: The name of the prop that provides the Airtable table ID

allOf.description: This prop is used to select a Discord channel.

appProp.description: The name of the app prop that provides Discord authentication

allOf.description: This prop is used to select multiple Discord channels.

appProp.description: The name of the app prop that provides Discord authentication

allOf.description: For Discord components, this prop can accept a Discord channel ID.
This prop can accept an integer value.

min.description: The minimum value for this integer prop.

max.description: The maximum value for this integer prop.

allOf.description: This prop can accept a set of key-value pairs.

ConfigurablePropStringFormat.description: The format of the string value. `file-ref` indicates a URL of a file or path to a file in the component's /tmp directory.

allOf.description: This prop can accept a string value.

secret.description: If true, this prop is a secret and should not be displayed in plain text.

allOf.description: This prop can accept an array of strings.

secret.description: If true, this prop is a secret and should not be displayed in plain text.

default.description: The default value for this prop

ConfigurableProp.description: A configuration or input field for a component. This is a discriminated union based on the type field.

value.description: The raw SQL query, as provided by the user

params.description: The list of parameters for the prepared statement

usePreparedStatements.description: Whether to use prepared statements for the query or not

ConfiguredProps.description: The configured properties of the component

ConfigurePropOptions.description: Available options (with labels) for the configured prop

ConfigurePropOpts.description: Request options for configuring a component's prop

version.description: Optional component version (in SemVer format, for example '1.0.0'), defaults to latest

prop_name.description: The name of the prop to configure

blocking.description: Whether this operation should block until completion

ConfigurePropResponse.description: Response received after configuring a component's prop

stringOptions.description: Available options for the configured prop

context.description: New context after configuring the prop

errors.description: Any errors that occurred during configuration

ConnectToken.description: An authentication token with a limited lifespan

CreateAccountOpts.description: Request object for creating an account

app_slug.description: The app slug for the account

cfmap_json.description: JSON string containing the custom fields mapping

account_id.description: An existing account ID to reconnect. When provided, the account's credentials are updated instead of creating a new account. Must belong to the same external user and project environment as the connect token, and match the app identified by app_slug.

CreateOAuthTokenOpts.description: Request object for creating an OAuth token

scope.description: Optional space-separated scopes for the access token. Defaults to `*`.

CreateOAuthTokenResponse.description: Response object for creating an OAuth token

CreateTokenOpts.description: Request object for creating a connect token

allowed_origins.description: List of allowed origins for CORS

error_redirect_uri.description: URI to redirect to on error

expires_in.description: Token TTL in seconds (max 14400 = 4 hours). Defaults to 4 hours if not specified.

external_user_id.description: Your end user ID, for whom you're creating the token

scope.description: Space-separated scopes to restrict token permissions. Defaults to 'connect:*' if not specified. See https://pipedream.com/docs/connect/api-reference/authentication#connect-token-scopes for more information.

success_redirect_uri.description: URI to redirect to on success

allow_progressive_scopes.description: When true, end users may authorize a subset of the app's OAuth scopes; only the app's functional scopes (needed for the post-OAuth test request) are enforced. Defaults to false.

CreateTokenResponse.description: Response received after creating a connect token

expires_at.description: The expiration time of the token in ISO 8601 format

DeleteTriggerOpts.description: Request options for deleting a deployed trigger

ignore_hook_errors.description: Whether to ignore errors during deactivation hook

id.description: The unique ID of the deployed component

component_id.description: The ID of the component that was deployed

component_key.description: The component key (name) that was deployed

configurable_props.description: The configurable properties of the component

active.description: Whether the deployed component is active

created_at.description: The timestamp when the component was deployed (epoch milliseconds)

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the component was last updated (epoch milliseconds)

name.description: The name of the deployed component

name_slug.description: The name slug of the deployed component

callback_observations.description: Callback observations for the deployed component

emit_on_deploy.description: Whether the trigger emits events during the deploy hook execution. When false, the $emit function is disabled during deploy hook execution. Defaults to true.

webhook_signing_key.description: The webhook signing key. Only returned for OAuth-authenticated requests when `webhook_url` is supplied.

DeployTriggerOpts.description: Request options for deploying a trigger

version.description: Optional trigger component version (in SemVer format, for example '1.0.0'), defaults to latest

workflow_id.description: Optional ID of a workflow to receive trigger events

webhook_url.description: Optional webhook URL to receive trigger events

emit_on_deploy.description: Whether the trigger should emit events during the deploy hook execution. Defaults to true if not specified.

DeployTriggerResponse.description: Response received after deploying a trigger

DynamicProps.description: Dynamic properties of a saved component

id.description: The unique ID of the dynamic prop

EmittedEvent.description: An event emitted by a trigger

ts.description: The event's timestamp in epoch milliseconds

EmitterType.description: The type of the event emitting interface

ErrorResponse.description: Error response returned by the API in case of an error

external_id.description: Your identifier for the external user

GetAccountsResponse.description: Response received when retrieving a list of accounts

GetAppResponse.description: Response received when retrieving a single app

GetAppsResponse.description: Response received when retrieving a list of apps

GetComponentResponse.description: Response received when retrieving a specific component

GetComponentsResponse.description: Response received when retrieving a list of components

GetTriggerEventsResponse.description: Response received when retrieving trigger events

GetTriggerResponse.description: Response received when retrieving a deployed trigger

GetTriggerWebhooksResponse.description: Response received when retrieving trigger webhooks

webhooks.description: Webhook objects for the configured URLs. `signing_key` is only returned for OAuth-authenticated requests.

GetTriggerWorkflowsResponse.description: Response received when retrieving trigger workflows

GetTriggersResponse.description: Response received when listing deployed triggers

id.description: The unique ID of the HTTP interface

created_at.description: The timestamp when the HTTP interface was created (epoch milliseconds)

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the HTTP interface was last updated (epoch milliseconds)

Project.description: Project that can be accessed via the Connect API

app_name.description: App name shown to Connect users

support_email.description: Support email configured for the project

connect_require_key_auth_test.description: Send a test request to the upstream API when adding Connect accounts for key-based apps

app_name.description: Display name for the Connect application

support_email.description: Support email displayed to end users

connect_require_key_auth_test.description: Send a test request to the upstream API when adding Connect accounts for key-based apps

app_name.description: Display name for the Connect application

support_email.description: Support email displayed to end users

connect_require_key_auth_test.description: Send a test request to the upstream API when adding Connect accounts for key-based apps

UpdateProjectLogoOpts.description: Payload to update a project's logo.

logo.description: Data URI containing the new Base64 encoded image

ListProjectsResponse.description: Response received when listing Connect projects

ListAppCategoriesResponse.description: Response received when listing app categories

Observation.description: Any logs produced during the configuration of the prop

ts.description: The time at which the log was produced, as milliseconds since the epoch

ObservationError.description: Details about an observed error message

stack.description: The stack trace of the error

start_cursor.description: Used to fetch the previous page of items

end_cursor.description: Used to fetch the next page of items

ProjectEnvironment.description: The environment in which the server client is running

ProjectInfoResponse.description: Response received when retrieving project info

id.description: ID of the app. Only applies for OAuth apps.

name_slug.description: The name slug of the target app

PropOption.description: A configuration option for a component's prop

label.description: The human-readable label for the option

PropOptionNested.description: A configuration option for a component's prop (nested under `__lv`)

PropOptionValue.description: The value of a prop option

ProxyResponse.description: The parsed response body from a proxied API request

ProxyResponseBinary.description: Binary response from the proxied API

ReloadPropsOpts.description: Request options for reloading a component's props when dealing with dynamic props

version.description: Optional component version (in SemVer format, for example '1.0.0'), defaults to latest

blocking.description: Whether this operation should block until completion

errors.description: Any errors that occurred during configuration

RunActionOpts.description: Request options for running an action

version.description: Optional action component version (in SemVer format, for example '1.0.0'), defaults to latest

RunActionOptsStashId.description: The ID of the File Stash to use for syncing the action's /tmp directory, or either `true` or 'NEW' to create a new stash

RunActionResponse.description: The response received after running an action. See https://pipedream.com/docs/components/api#returning-data-from-steps for more details.

exports.description: The key-value pairs resulting from calls to `$.export`

os.description: Any logs produced during the execution of the action

ret.description: The value returned by the action

StartConnectOpts.description: Options for starting the connection process

iframe.description: ID of the iframe element to use

StashId.description: The ID of the File Stash

id.description: The unique ID of the timer interface

created_at.description: The timestamp when the timer interface was created (epoch milliseconds)

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the timer interface was last updated (epoch milliseconds)

UpdateTriggerOpts.description: Request options for updating a trigger

active.description: Whether the trigger should be active

emit_on_deploy.description: Whether the trigger should emit events during deployment

UpdateTriggerWebhooksOpts.description: Request options for updating webhooks that listen to trigger events

webhook_urls.description: Array of webhook URLs to set

UpdateTriggerWorkflowsOpts.description: Request options for updating workflows that listen to trigger events

workflow_ids.description: Array of workflow IDs to set

TriggerWebhook.description: A trigger webhook object. `signing_key` is only returned for OAuth-authenticated requests.

id.description: The unique ID of the webhook

signing_key.description: The webhook signing key, returned only for OAuth-authenticated requests

signing_key_set.description: Whether a signing key has been set for this webhook

id.description: The unique ID of the webhook

signing_key_set.description: Whether a signing key has been set for this webhook

created_at.description: The time the webhook was created, in epoch seconds

updated_at.description: The time the webhook was last updated, in epoch seconds

WebhookWithOptionalSigningKey.description: A webhook object that may include the signing key. The signing key is returned when a webhook is created, but omitted when an existing webhook is updated.

id.description: The unique ID of the webhook

signing_key.description: The webhook signing key, when returned by the endpoint

signing_key_set.description: Whether a signing key has been set for this webhook

created_at.description: The time the webhook was created, in epoch seconds

updated_at.description: The time the webhook was last updated, in epoch seconds

WebhookWithSigningKey.description: A webhook object including the signing key

id.description: The unique ID of the webhook

signing_key_set.description: Whether a signing key has been set for this webhook

created_at.description: The time the webhook was created, in epoch seconds

updated_at.description: The time the webhook was last updated, in epoch seconds

GetWebhookResponse.description: Response received when retrieving a webhook

GetWebhookWithSigningKeyResponse.description: Response received when retrieving a webhook with its signing key

SetWebhookResponse.description: Response received when creating or updating a webhook

SetWebhookOpts.description: Request options for setting a webhook URL

ValidateTokenResponse.description: Response received when validating a connect token

error_redirect_uri.description: URI to redirect to on error

success.description: Whether the token validation was successful

success_redirect_uri.description: URI to redirect to on success
